description_long: This is a roughly hemispherical, hand-formed bowl with an irregular, organic shape and a visibly coarse, textured surface. The bowl appears to be made from unglazed, reddish-brown clay, with natural color variations and some lighter patches. Its rim is uneven and jagged, with a large section missing, giving it a broken and ancient appearance. The interior is similarly rough and unpolished, showing signs of wear and age. On one side, there is a white label with handwritten text, suggesting it may be an archaeological artifact or museum piece. The overall form is utilitarian but heavily worn, with a distinctly primitive and historical character.
description: A broken, hand-formed clay bowl with a rough, reddish-brown surface and an irregular, jagged rim. It features a white label with handwritten text and shows significant signs of age and wear.
description_view_0: The bowl is seen from the front, highlighting its jagged, broken rim and rough, uneven surface texture.
description_view_1: This angled view emphasizes the bowl's irregular shape and the extent of the missing rim section.
description_view_2: The side with the white label is visible, showing the handwritten text and more of the bowl's coarse, weathered surface.
description_view_3: The interior of the bowl is most visible here, revealing its rough, unpolished texture and the full extent of the breakage.
attribute_location: archaeological site, museum, or historical display
attribute_context_of_use: serving or holding food, or as an artifact for study or display

license_info.creator_display_name: The Royal Museums of Art and History
